macro_segments.title: Communal Obligations for Courtyard Gates and City Walls

micro_segments.title: Introduction to Communal Responsibility and the Obligation to Build a Guard's Gate for the Courtyard
Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel's View: Not All Courtyards Require a Guard's Gate
Transition from Courtyard Gates to City Walls and Communal Obligations
Duration of Residency Required to Become a Member of the City and Pay Taxes

macro_segments.title: The Gemara's Discussion of Guard's Gates and the Exclusion of the Poor

micro_segments.title: The Story of a Pious Sage and Elijah: The Guard's Gate Prevents Poor from Receiving Charity
Multiple Resolutions to the Tension Between Guard's Gates and Access for the Poor

macro_segments.title: The Rabbinic Discussion of Taxation: Per Person vs. Proportional to Wealth and Benefit

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Elazar Asks Rabbi Yochanan: Do People Pay Per Person or According to Wealth?
Second Query: Is Taxation Based on Proximity to the Wall (Benefit) or Income?
Tosafot's Analysis: The Interaction Between Ability to Pay and Benefit Derived

macro_segments.title: Exemption of Rabbis from Protection-Related Taxes

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehuda Nesi'ah Attempts to Tax the Rabbis; Resh Lakish Objects Using Midrashic Proof
Rav Nachman bar Rav Chisda Places a Poll Tax; Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak Objects with Scriptural Arguments
Biblical and Prophetic Verses: Rabbis Are Protected by God and Should Be Exempt from Taxes
Distinction Between Protection-Related Taxes (Exempt) and Benefit-Based Taxes (Required)

macro_segments.title: The Story of Rabbi and His Warehouse During Famine: Torah Scholars vs. Illiterate Poor

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Opens His Warehouse; Yonatan ben Amram Sneaks In Disguised as an Illiterate Person
Rabbi's Discomfort Resolved When He Learns Yonatan Refused to Benefit from Torah Honor
The Gemara's Explanation: Rabbi's View That Calamities Come Because of the Illiterate
Story of Rabbi Refusing to Tax the Rabbis for the King's Crown; Half the City Abandons It

macro_segments.title: Duration of Residency for Different Communal Obligations and Charitable Contributions

micro_segments.title: The Case of Transient Caravan Participants and the Thirty-Day vs. Twelve-Month Distinction
Graduated Timeline for Communal Obligations: Tamchui, Kuppa, Clothing, Burial, and City Walls
All Members Including Orphans Must Contribute to City Walls; Rabbis Exempted

macro_segments.title: Orphans and Charitable Obligations: The Limit of Enforced Contributions

micro_segments.title: Rabba Places a Charity Tax on Orphans; Abaye Objects Using a Baraita About Pidyon Shvuyim
Rabba's Justification: Contributing to Charity Raises Orphans' Status in Society
Ifra Hormiz Sends Money to Rav Yosef for a Great Mitzvah; Abaye Infers Pidyon Shvuyim is Paramount

topical_tags.term: היזק ראיה (hezek re'iyah) – invasion of privacy
כופין אותו (kofin oso) – you force him
Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel – exemption from obligations
תנ״ך / ספר / משנה / גמרא (Tanach, Sefer, Mishnah, Gemara) – levels of Torah study
לפי נפשות (lefi nefashot) – per person tax
לפי שבח ממון (lefi shevach mamon) – proportional to wealth/benefit
לפי קירוב בתים (lefi keruv batim) – based on proximity to wall
תוספות (Tosafot) – medieval commentary on benefit vs. ability to pay
סכנת נפשות (sakanat nefashot) – danger to life
תלמיד חכם / רבנן (talmid chacham / rabbanan) – Torah scholar / Rabbis
אליהו הנביא (Eliyahu Hanavi) – the prophet Elijah
כריית בור (kriyat bur) – digging a well
כרגא / כריית (karga / krit) – poll tax / tax for well-digging
עם הארץ (am ha'aretz) – illiterate person, not learned in Torah
כנס ישראל (Knesset Yisrael) – congregation of Israel
תורה מגינה (Torah magina) – Torah as protective force
דרכי שלום (darchei shalom) – ways of peace
ראשך / נפקא מינה (rosh / nafka mina) – head / practical difference
